sources.title: California Department of Industrial Relations — Paid Sick Leave FAQs (Cal Lab Code §§ 245-249, SB 616)
data_extracted: Minimum 40 hours / 5 days per year (SB 616 raised from 24 hours effective 1 Jan 2024)
Accrual rate 1 hour per 30 hours worked
Accrual cap 80 hours / 10 days; annual use cap 40 hours / 5 days
Applies to any employer with employees working 30+ days in California (no headcount threshold)
Permitted uses including family member care, qualifying violence, jury duty (2025), preventive care
Designated person added by AB 1041 effective 1 January 2023
PTO bank may substitute if meets all statutory minimums

sources.title: California Employment Development Department — Paid Family Leave Programme
data_extracted: Up to 8 weeks PFL in a 12-month period
Minimum weekly benefit $50; maximum weekly benefit $1,765 in 2026
Earnings threshold $300 in SDI contributions during 18-month base period
Three claim types: bonding, care, military assist
PFL does NOT provide job protection — separate from CFRA/FMLA/PDL

sources.title: EDD 2026 SDI/PFL Rate Notice (Cal Unemp Ins Code § 984; SB 951)
data_extracted: 2026 SDI rate 1.3% (up from 1.2% in 2025)
No taxable wage cap (removed 1 January 2024 under SB 951)
Maximum weekly PFL benefit $1,765 (up from $1,681 in 2025)
State Average Weekly Wage 2026 $1,789
Funded entirely through employee payroll deduction

sources.title: Cal Gov Code § 12945.2 — California Family Rights Act (FindLaw + California Legislative Information)
data_extracted: Employer threshold 5+ employees since 1 January 2021 (SB 1383, Stats. 2020, ch. 86)
Employee eligibility 12 months service + 1,250 hours in preceding 12 months
12 weeks job-protected leave per 12-month period
Family definition broader than FMLA — includes parent-in-law, grandparent, grandchild, sibling, designated person (AB 1041, 2022)
Runs concurrently with FMLA when both apply

sources.title: Cal Gov Code § 12945 + 2 Cal Code Regs § 11042 — Pregnancy Disability Leave
data_extracted: Up to 4 months leave per pregnancy
5+ employee threshold
No tenure or hours-worked eligibility requirement
Mandatory health-insurance continuation at same level/conditions throughout (up to 4 months)
Stacks with CFRA: PDL for disability period + CFRA for baby bonding = ~7 months total at 5+ employer

sources.title: San Francisco Paid Sick Leave Ordinance + multi-city California ordinance summary (Six Fifty Compliance, A Better Balance)
data_extracted: SF accrual cap 40 hrs (<10 employees) / 72 hrs (10+ employees)
California state 80-hour cap preempts SF's 72 — employer must allow 80 hr accrual to comply with both
LA, Oakland, Berkeley, San Diego, Emeryville each have distinct ordinances
Employer applies the most-generous rule of state / local / contract on each substantive point

sources.title: California compliance cache (Teamed sprint, fetched 21 May 2026)
data_extracted: Entity status own_entity — Teamed US Inc., Delaware
SDI 2026 rate 1.3% confirmed against EDD December 2025 announcement (resolved page-specific guidance discrepancy of 1.2%)
Worker classification ABC test under AB5 — cross-reference for sibling pages

sources.title: AB 1041 (Stats. 2022, ch. 748) — Designated Person expansion of CFRA and PSL family definitions
data_extracted: Effective 1 January 2023
Adds 'designated person' to family definition under both CFRA and PSL
Employee designates the person at the time of leave

compliance_notes.pullquote_provenance: MI MCP unreliable per Colorado page experience; pullquote anonymised to 'Teamed Legal Operations' with no <cite> line and no named individual. Tom Price-Daniel tagline-close stays as brand voice (exempt from MI provenance per pullquote rule).
compliance_notes.secondary_cta_destinations: Both secondary CTAs link to real Teamed tool destinations: masthead secondary points to Employer Cost Calculator, tagline-close secondary points to Crossover Calculator.
compliance_notes.factual_uncertainty_resolved: SDI rate 1.3% in 2026 verified against EDD December 2025 announcement, overriding the 1.2% reference in page-specific guidance. No remaining factual conflicts.
compliance_notes.city_count_lower_bound: Page lists 6 cities with named ordinances (SF, LA, Oakland, Berkeley, San Diego, Emeryville) and notes 'at least six' — there are additional industry-specific ordinances in Long Beach, Santa Monica, and LA County (hotels, fast food) that are referenced but not detailed.
